mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward returns for the S&P 500 over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in 60% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and rising retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand growth, driving corporate earnings expectations higher and producing positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate a positive mean forward return over 60 days, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, rising industrial production indicates capacity utilization improvements that outpace inflationary pressures, yielding positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns specifically during post-shock recovery windows, with out-of-sample R-squared dropping below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect immediate construction sector activity and consumer confidence, generating short-term positive momentum in equity markets over a five-day window.
failure_condition: HOUST increases consistently precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns, or the signal produces a negative information coefficient over rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0 percent, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 65% of periods following CPIAUCSL spikes above the threshold, invalidating the tightening transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment rates indicates healthy job creation without wage-price spiral risks, supporting positive monthly equity performance.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and flat UNRATE yields negative forward returns, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, declining retail sales signal consumer exhaustion and impending earnings downgrades, triggering immediate negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Negative RSAFS revisions fail to predict short-term equity declines during late-cycle phases, with forward returns remaining positive in over 70% of triggered cases.
